description: Kian (کیان) was the most influential intellectual magazine of the pre-Khatami reform era, founded in November 1991 by publisher Mostafa Rokh-Sefat with [Mashallah Shamsolvaezin](/people/mashallah-shamsolvaezin) as editor. Its circle (*halgheh-ye Kian*) included Abdolkarim Soroush, [Saeed Hajjarian](/people/saeed-hajjarian), [Akbar Ganji](/people/akbar-ganji), and others who became the intellectual architects of the reform movement. Kian emerged when Khatami's 1992 resignation from the Culture Ministry forced Soroush's circle out of *Kayhan-e Farhangi*. Closed by judicial order in 1998.
